vineyards.name: Chateau Margaux
vineyards.tasting_program: By appointment only, Monday to Friday; closed weekends, August, public holidays and harvest. Tasting for professionals only.
vineyards.description: Premier Cru Classe Bordeaux at Margaux AOC, estate founded 1572, now owned by the Mentzelopoulos family. Cabernet-dominant blends with 18 to 24 months in new oak barrel ageing.

vineyards.name: Chateau Lafite Rothschild
vineyards.owner: Domaines Barons de Rothschild (Lafite); Saskia de Rothschild, chair
vineyards.tasting_program: Currently closed for renovations until 2026. Alternative visits at Chateau Duhart-Milon.
vineyards.description: First-growth Pauillac estate with 112 hectares of Cabernet-dominant vines. Owned by the Rothschild family; fully organic since 2024 with Saskia de Rothschild at the helm.

vineyards.name: Chateau Latour
vineyards.tasting_program: By appointment only for professionals; visiting restricted.
vineyards.description: First-growth Pauillac estate with 78 hectares at the southeastern tip of Pauillac commune. Owned by Groupe Artemis since 1993; withdrew from en primeur system in 2012.

vineyards.name: Chateau Mouton Rothschild
vineyards.classification: Premier Cru Classe (1855 classification; elevated from 2nd growth in 1973)
vineyards.owner: Rothschild family (Camille, Philippe, and Julien Sereys de Rothschild)
vineyards.tasting_program: Visits by appointment; museum of wine in art open to visitors.
vineyards.description: Pauillac first-growth elevated from second in 1973, famous for its artist-label series. 83 hectares planted 81% Cabernet Sauvignon; Jean-Emmanuel Danjoy is technical director since 2020.

vineyards.name: Chateau Haut-Brion
vineyards.classification: Premier Cru Classe (1855 classification); Cru Classe de Graves
vineyards.tasting_program: Visits by appointment only for professionals.
vineyards.description: The only non-Medoc first-growth in the 1855 classification, estate since 1521 in Pessac. Merlot-driven blends from iron-rich gravelly soils; Domaine Clarence Dillon owner.

vineyards.name: Chateau Cheval Blanc
vineyards.classification: Saint-Emilion Grand Cru (withdrew from Premier Grand Cru Classe A classification as of 2022)
vineyards.owner: Bernard Arnault (LVMH) and Albert Frère family
vineyards.tasting_program: Visits by appointment; contact through official site.
vineyards.description: Iconic Saint-Emilion estate on 39 hectares with an unusual 52% Cabernet Franc dominant blend. Withdrew from the Saint-Emilion classification in 2022; co-owned by LVMH.

vineyards.name: Petrus
vineyards.classification: Pomerol AOC (unclassified; Pomerol has no official classification)
vineyards.tasting_program: Not open to the public. No visits or tastings accepted.
vineyards.description: The world's most prestigious Pomerol, just 11.4 hectares of 100% Merlot on blue clay. Production of 30,000 bottles per year; Olivier Berrouet winemaker since 2008.

vineyards.name: Le Pin
vineyards.tasting_program: Not open to the public.
vineyards.description: Ultra-micro Pomerol estate of just 2.7 hectares producing 400 to 600 cases of 100% Merlot per year. One of the most expensive wines in Bordeaux; first vintage 1979.

vineyards.name: Vieux Chateau Certan
vineyards.tasting_program: Visits by appointment.
vineyards.description: Pomerol's counterpart to Petrus, owned by the Thienpont family since 1924. 14-hectare contiguous plot planted 60% Merlot, 30% Cabernet Franc; 100th anniversary celebrated in 2024.

vineyards.name: Chateau d'Yquem
vineyards.tasting_program: Tours and tastings available by appointment; booking through official site.
vineyards.description: The only Sauternes Premier Cru Superieur, producing botrytised Semillon-Sauvignon Blanc of extraordinary longevity. LVMH-owned; Sandrine Garbay winemaker; average 10 hl/ha yield.

vineyards.name: Chateau Ausone
vineyards.classification: Saint-Emilion Grand Cru (withdrew from Premier Grand Cru Classe A classification as of 2022)
vineyards.tasting_program: By appointment only.
vineyards.description: Historic Saint-Emilion estate of 7 hectares planted 55% Cabernet Franc on limestone plateau; family-owned Vauthier; ultra-limited annual production of around 1,500 cases.

vineyards.name: Chateau Cos d'Estournel
vineyards.tasting_program: Visits and tastings available through La Maison d'Estournel hotel.
vineyards.description: Saint-Estephe second growth with 91 hectares, famous for pagoda-adorned winery. Fully organic since 2024; Michel Reybier owner; produces red, second wine, and a white Sauvignon-Semillon blend.

vineyards.name: Chateau Ducru-Beaucaillou
vineyards.winemaker: Bruno Borie (with consultants Eric and Jacques Boissenot)
vineyards.tasting_program: By appointment only.
vineyards.description: Saint-Julien second growth with 75 hectares on the Gironde riverside. Bruno Borie owner and manager; 70% Cabernet Sauvignon blend with 18 months in oak barrels.

vineyards.name: Chateau Lynch-Bages
vineyards.tasting_program: Visits, tastings and accommodation at Chateau Cordeillan-Bages; village dining at Cafe Lavinal in Bages.
vineyards.description: Pauillac fifth growth that consistently overdelivers, with 90 hectares planted 67% Cabernet Sauvignon. Cazes-family owned since 1939; 18 months in 75% new oak barrels.

vineyards.name: Chateau Pichon-Longueville Baron
vineyards.tasting_program: Visits available; contact chateau for details.
vineyards.description: Pauillac second growth of 73 hectares owned by AXA Millesimes. Alexandra Lebossé winemaker since 2017; produces grand vin, Les Griffons second wine, and a white from 2022.

vineyards.name: Chateau Pichon Longueville Comtesse de Lalande
vineyards.tasting_program: Visits available by appointment.
vineyards.description: Pauillac second growth adjacent to Chateau Latour. 89 hectares with unusually high 32% Merlot for Left Bank; Louis Roederer Champagne owner since 2006.

vineyards.name: Chateau Pontet-Canet
vineyards.tasting_program: Visits and tastings available by appointment.
vineyards.description: Pauillac fifth growth leading Bordeaux biodynamics; 81 hectares certified Demeter since 2014 and Biodyvin since 2010. Tesseron family owners; Mathieu Bessonnet technical director since 2020.

vineyards.name: Chateau Palmer
vineyards.tasting_program: Visits by appointment.
vineyards.description: Margaux third growth frequently outperforming its classification; 66 hectares certified 100% biodynamic since 2017. Thomas Duroux managing director since 2004.

vineyards.name: Chateau La Mission Haut-Brion
vineyards.tasting_program: Visits by appointment only.
vineyards.description: Graves Cru Classe adjacent to Haut-Brion in Talence, sharing the same Domaine Clarence Dillon ownership. 23 hectares producing powerful, structured Cabernet-Merlot blends.

vineyards.name: Chateau Leoville-Barton
vineyards.tasting_program: By appointment for professionals and private visits.
vineyards.description: Saint-Julien second growth family-owned without interruption since 1826. 48 hectares planted predominantly Cabernet Sauvignon; known for exceptional value among classed growths.

vineyards.name: Chateau Leoville-Las Cases
vineyards.tasting_program: By appointment for professionals.
vineyards.description: Saint-Julien second growth known as the 'super second' of the appellation. 97 hectares with the famous walled Grand Clos enclos; Delon family ownership.

vineyards.name: Chateau Angelus
vineyards.classification: Saint-Emilion Grand Cru (withdrew from Premier Grand Cru Classe A in 2022)
vineyards.owner: Bouard de Laforest family (Stephanie de Bouard-Rivoal)
vineyards.tasting_program: Visits available by appointment.
vineyards.description: Iconic Saint-Emilion estate known for its bell-tower icon. 27 hectares of Merlot-Cabernet Franc; organic certified; withdrew from 2022 classification; Stephanie de Bouard-Rivoal leads.

vineyards.name: Chateau Pavie
vineyards.classification: Saint-Emilion Premier Grand Cru Classe A (2022 classification)
vineyards.tasting_program: Hotel de Pavie on-site with two Michelin-starred restaurant.
vineyards.description: Saint-Emilion Premier Grand Cru Classe A, 37 hectares on the famous Pavie hill. Gerard Perse owner; Hotel de Pavie on-site features chef Yannick Alleno Michelin-starred restaurant.

vineyards.name: Chateau Troplong Mondot
vineyards.classification: Saint-Emilion Premier Grand Cru Classe B (2022 classification)
vineyards.tasting_program: The Keys hotel with Michelin-starred restaurant Les Belles Perdrix on-site.
vineyards.description: Saint-Emilion Premier Grand Cru Classe B on the highest hill of the appellation. 33 hectares, organic certified; The Keys boutique hotel with Michelin restaurant Les Belles Perdrix on-site.

vineyards.name: Chateau Canon
vineyards.classification: Saint-Emilion Premier Grand Cru Classe B (2022 classification)
vineyards.tasting_program: By appointment only.
vineyards.description: Saint-Emilion Premier Grand Cru Classe B owned by Chanel SAS since 1996. 22 hectares with limestone plateau parcels; organic certified; Nicolas Audebert director.

vineyards.name: Chateau Pape Clement
vineyards.address: 216 avenue du Docteur Nancel Penard, 33600 Pessac, France
vineyards.tasting_program: Hotel and restaurant on-site; visits and tastings available.
vineyards.description: Pessac-Leognan Cru Classe de Graves with origins traced to 1300. 32 hectares; Bernard Magrez owner; truffle-enhanced restaurant and botanical gardens featuring a 1,000-year-old olive tree.

verified.address_quoted: 216 avenue du Docteur Nancel Penard, 33600 Pessac, France

vineyards.name: Chateau Smith Haut Lafitte
vineyards.address: Chateau Smith Haut Lafitte, 33650 Martillac, France
vineyards.tasting_program: Les Sources de Caudalie hotel with Vinothérapie Spa and Michelin-starred restaurant; open to hotel guests and visitors.
vineyards.description: Pessac-Leognan Cru Classe de Graves owned by the Cathiards since 1990. 67 hectares including white wine production; Les Sources de Caudalie luxury spa resort on-site.

vineyards.name: Chateau Rieussec
vineyards.tasting_program: Visits and tastings available by appointment.
vineyards.description: Sauternes Premier Cru adjacent to d'Yquem, owned by Domaines Barons de Rothschild since 1984. 93 hectares of Semillon-dominant botrytised dessert wine production.

vineyards.name: Chateau Lafaurie-Peyraguey
vineyards.tasting_program: Lalique hotel with two Michelin-starred restaurant; open to hotel guests.
vineyards.description: Sauternes Premier Cru within a fairytale 1618 castle; 18 hectares of enclosed vineyard. Lalique luxury hotel with two-Michelin-starred restaurant and 350,000-bottle cellar.

vineyards.name: Chateau Meyney
vineyards.tasting_program: Visits by appointment.
vineyards.description: Saint-Estephe Cru Bourgeois Exceptionnel with 51 hectares and a former Celestine priory. Reliable, full-bodied Cabernet-dominant style representing excellent appellation value.

vineyards.name: Chateau Clinet
vineyards.tasting_program: By appointment only.
vineyards.description: Pomerol estate of 9 hectares dominated by old-vine Merlot. Ronan Laborde family-owned; 1998 vintage scored 100 points from Wine Advocate; old-vine character a distinguishing feature.

vineyards.name: Chateau Lafleur
vineyards.tasting_program: By appointment only.
vineyards.description: Tiny 4.5-hectare Pomerol estate considered peer to Petrus. Unusual equal split of Merlot and Cabernet Franc; Guinaudeau family owners; biodynamic farming since 2014.

vineyards.name: Chateau Beau-Sejour Becot
vineyards.classification: Saint-Emilion Premier Grand Cru Classe B (2022 classification)
vineyards.tasting_program: Visits and tastings available; contact chateau.
vineyards.description: Saint-Emilion Premier Grand Cru Classe B on 16.5 hectares including ancient underground cellars carved from limestone. Becot family-managed; organic certified.

vineyards.name: Chateau Figeac
vineyards.classification: Saint-Emilion Premier Grand Cru Classe A (2022 classification)
vineyards.tasting_program: Visits by appointment.
vineyards.description: Saint-Emilion Premier Grand Cru Classe A elevated in 2022 reclassification. Unusual for Right Bank with an equal-thirds Cabernet Sauvignon, Cabernet Franc, Merlot blend on 40 hectares of gravelly soils.

vineyards.name: Chateau Larose Trintaudon
vineyards.tasting_program: Cellar door open Monday to Friday with tastings.
vineyards.description: The largest Cru Bourgeois in the Medoc at 170 hectares. Reliable Cabernet-Merlot blends from Haut-Medoc with good fruit and structure at accessible price points.

vineyards.name: Chateau Camensac
vineyards.tasting_program: Visits by appointment.
vineyards.description: Haut-Medoc fifth growth of 65 hectares, offering classed-growth quality at accessible prices. Merlaut family management bringing consistent improvement since 2005.

vineyards.name: Chateau Bauduc
vineyards.tasting_program: Farmhouse accommodation; vineyard visits; winery tours by arrangement.
vineyards.description: English-owned Entre-Deux-Mers estate known for crisp whites and reliable Bordeaux reds. 40 hectares; farmhouse accommodation; award-winning wines at accessible price points.

vineyards.name: Chateau de la Riviere
vineyards.address: Chateau de la Riviere, 33126 La Rivière, France
vineyards.tasting_program: Visits, wine-and-cheese tastings, and cave-tour experiences available.
vineyards.description: Fronsac's flagship estate with 60 hectares and a spectacular 16th-century underground cave network. Known for structured, Merlot-dominant wines with Right Bank elegance.

vineyards.name: Chateau Roc de Cambes
vineyards.address: Chateau Roc de Cambes, 33710 Bourg-sur-Gironde, France
vineyards.tasting_program: By appointment only.
vineyards.description: Cotes de Bourg cult wine from Francois Mitjavile, creator of Le Tertre-Roteboeuf. 10 hectares on limestone slopes; Merlot-led blends with remarkable concentration and minerality.

vineyards.name: Chateau Moulin Pey-Labrie
vineyards.tasting_program: Visits and tastings available by appointment.
vineyards.description: Canon-Fronsac producer representing the best of this underrated Right Bank appellation. 7 hectares of organic Merlot-Cabernet Franc on clay-limestone soils.

vineyards.name: Chateau Chasse-Spleen
vineyards.tasting_program: Visits and tastings available.
vineyards.description: Moulis-en-Medoc Cru Bourgeois Exceptionnel with 100 hectares. Named for Baudelaire's 'chase away the blues'; Cabernet-dominant with excellent value-to-quality ratio.

vineyards.name: Chateau Sociando-Mallet
vineyards.tasting_program: Cellar visits available by appointment.
vineyards.description: Haut-Medoc Cru Bourgeois that many consider above its classification. 85 hectares of Cabernet-dominant vines; Jean Gautreau's uncompromising quality standard since 1969.
